commit.message:
fix(kick): parse emote tokens, show badges as text chips, add kick CDN to image allowlist

Three Kick rendering issues fixed:

1. Emotes: [emote:ID:name] tokens in message text were displayed as literal
   text. Now parsed into positioned Emote objects with URLs pointing to
   https://files.kick.com/emotes/{ID}/fullsize and cleaned text with just
   the emote name for position tracking. The frontend renderer already
   handles positioned emotes correctly.

2. Badges: icon_url was always empty (Kick has no public badge image CDN).
   Frontend now renders a text chip fallback when icon_url is absent,
   showing the badge name (e.g. "subscriber") as a small gray label.
   Also fixed duplicate badge deduplication and prefer structured badge
   data from raw_message over tags.

3. next.config.js: added files.kick.com to allowed image domains so
   Next.js Image component can load Kick emote images.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add packages.txt for Streamlit Cloud system font dependency

Streamlit Community Cloud (Linux) has no fonts installed by default.
This installs fonts-liberation which provides metrically-compatible
substitutes for Arial, Times New Roman, and Courier New — required
for font embedding to work in the PDF accessibility pipeline.

Without this, all font embedding silently fails on the deployed app.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Add fonttools to requirements.txt (was missing — caused silent font
  embedding failures when installed without the venv's weasyprint dep)

  variants so font embedding works on Linux/Docker where MS fonts are
  absent (apt-get install fonts-liberation)
- Log WARNING with install hint when a font file cannot be found on disk
- Make fontTools ImportError visible as ERROR + print (was silent WARNING)
- Fix validator.py subprocess encoding to utf-8 with errors=replace to
  prevent crash on non-UTF-8 veraPDF output

* refactor: separate concerns and add SQS buffering

- Split monolithic handler into focused modules: handler, message-processor,
  telegram-client, config, message-validator, and authorizer

- Add Biome linter/formatter, TypeScript strict mode, path aliases
- Add Vitest test suite with 52 tests across 6 files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: add tofu:plan script and rename tofu:deploy to tofu:apply

* fix: bundle undici to fix Lambda runtime import error

undici is not available as a standalone package in Node.js 24 Lambda
runtime, causing ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND on cold start.

* ci: trigger notification test

* ci: test notification delivery

* ci: test notification after code update

* fix: replace undici with native fetch for Lambda compatibility

undici cannot be bundled (CJS require in ESM context) or externalized
(not available as standalone package in Lambda Node 24 runtime).
Native fetch is available in Node 24 and requires no dependencies.

* style: fix biome formatting in telegram-client test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Workflow improvements: 5 reliability & maintainability fixes

1. fetch_races.py: Add track_type column to tracks schema and derive
   it from ESPN's shape field (superspeedway/road_course/short_track/
   intermediate). Prevents crash when load_segment.py queries track_type
   on a freshly-built database.

2. segment.json + report.py: Move USER_TEAM out of source code into
   segment.json. No more editing Python to change your team name.

3. main.py + fetch_races.py: Replace single-retry API logic with
   exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s) across up to 4 attempts.

4. update.bat: Add if-errorlevel guards after every step so failures
   abort the pipeline immediately instead of cascading silently.
   git commit exit code 1 (nothing to commit) is treated as OK.

5. utils.py (new): Extract shared match_driver() and fuzzy_similarity()
   from load_segment.py and fetch_teams.py into a single module.
   Removes ~20 lines of duplicated fuzzy-matching logic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: parallelize dispatch generation in cron to avoid timeout

Sequential for-await loop took ~84s (12 MAGI × ~7s each), exceeding
Vercel's 60s function limit. Replaced with Promise.all so all dispatches
stream concurrently — total time drops to ~8-10s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: failure sound on no-file-found scan (#16)

## Summary
- Plays the system Exclamation sound
(`winsound.MessageBeep(MB_ICONEXCLAMATION)`) when a scanned barcode has
no matching file in the folder
- No popup or user interaction — scanner can keep working immediately
- Other failure paths (no folder, no printer, print error) already
produce audio via their `messagebox` dialogs and are unchanged
- `winsound` is stdlib; no new dependency

## Test plan
- [x] Scan a barcode that does not match any file in the folder —
exclamation sound plays, log shows error, no dialog appears
- [x] Scan a valid barcode — prints normally, no sound
- [x] Trigger a print error (e.g. disconnect printer) — existing error
dialog + its beep still works

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(audio): clamp active region boundaries to actual audio duration

Move computed_duration_ms calculation before active-region extraction so
it can be passed to active_regions_from_frames.  Region start/end are
now clamped to the file's duration, preventing regions that extend past
the last sample when the final frame is partial.  Zero-length regions
that result from clamping are dropped.

fix: add scriptPubKey to placeholder UTXO to resolve 'Address is required' error

TransactionBuilder.verifyUTXOValidity() requires utxo.scriptPubKey to be set,
and generatePsbtInputExtended reads scriptPubKey.hex to build the witnessUtxo.
Derive the P2TR scriptPubKey hex from the wallet's bech32 address via fromBech32.

commit.message:
Task 4.6: add Prettier, Husky hooks, and lint-staged (#19)

* Task 4.6: add Prettier, Husky hooks, and lint-staged

- Add Prettier 3.8.1 with .prettierrc (semi, singleQuote, trailingComma all,
  printWidth 100); run format to apply Prettier style to existing files
- Add eslint-config-prettier 10.1.8; replace @stylistic/eslint-plugin in
  eslint.config.ts so Prettier owns formatting and ESLint owns correctness
- Add Husky 9.1.7 at repo root with prepare script; pre-commit runs
  lint-staged, pre-push runs pnpm coverage and checks TS types drift
- Add lint-staged 16.3.3 in apps/frontend; staged *.{ts,vue} files get
  oxlint + eslint + prettier, staged *.{css,json,md,yaml} get prettier
- Update PLAN.md: mark 4.5 complete, mark 4.6 in-progress

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix pre-push hook: single cd to avoid repeated directory change

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add .prettierignore; restore api.gen.ts to canonical generated format

Prettier reformatted api.gen.ts in the previous commit, but pnpm generate:types
regenerates it with openapi-typescript's own formatting. The pre-push drift check
correctly caught the mismatch.

Fix: exclude generated files from Prettier (src/types/api.gen.ts, pnpm-lock.yaml)
and re-run generate:types to restore the canonical format.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Move lint-staged config to its own lint-staged.config.ts file

commit.message:
feat(workspaces): add @mastra/agentfs workspace filesystem provider (#13450)

## Description

Add a new workspace filesystem provider backed by AgentFS, which stores
files in a Turso/SQLite database via the agentfs-sdk. This gives Mastra
agents persistent, database-backed file storage that survives across
sessions — an alternative to the existing local and S3 filesystem
providers.

The provider implements the full WorkspaceFilesystem interface:
- File operations: read, write, append, delete, copy, move
- Directory operations: mkdir, rmdir, readdir (with recursive support)
- Path operations: exists, stat
- Read-only mode enforcement
- Automatic parent directory creation on write
- Error mapping from AgentFS/POSIX codes to Mastra workspace errors

Package: @mastra/agentfs (workspaces/agentfs/)
- Peer dependency on @mastra/core >=1.4.0
- Uses agentfs-sdk ^0.6.0
- Supports three initialization modes: agentId, explicit path, or
pre-opened AgentFS instance

Also includes an example project (examples/agentfs-workspace/) with:
- A standalone filesystem demo exercising all operations
- An agent demo showing workspace tools auto-injected into an agent

Mostly generated with Claude Code, with some minor manual touch-ups.

## Type of Change

New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)

## Checklist

- [x] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation (if
applicable)
- [x] I have added tests that prove my fix is effective or that my
feature works


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Added AgentFSFilesystem workspace provider for persistent,
database-backed file storage (Turso/SQLite).
* Added example workspace with runnable demos showcasing filesystem
operations and agent integration.

* **Documentation**
* Added comprehensive provider reference, usage examples, and sidebar
entry.
  * Added README and changelog entries for the new provider.

* **Tests**
  * Added unit and integration tests validating filesystem behavior.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

---------

fix: emit vp() phase variables in AC Sweep print commands (#738) (#803)

The netlist generator was only emitting v(node) print commands for AC
Sweep analysis, but ngspice AC results contain complex-valued vectors
that need both magnitude v() and phase vp() to be printed separately.
The result parser already expected both columns but never received phase
data, causing incomplete AC sweep results.

feat: pipeline quality gates — structural checks, LLM eval, SCOPE approval, pause/resume

Quality gates between pipeline stages prevent bad artifacts from propagating:
- Structural gates per stage (length, keyword, file-path checks)
- LLM judge for REVIEW stage (stateless haiku, score 0.0-1.0, threshold 0.7)
- SCOPE always pauses for user approval via ask_free_text
- Retry-then-escalate: gate failure → 1 retry with feedback → user escalation
- Pause/resume support: manage_pipeline pause/resume actions + wait_if_paused
- Always-gist: every artifact gets a gist URL stored in pipeline_artifacts
- Combined multi-file gist at pipeline completion
- pipeline_status tool for Enki to query pipeline health on demand
- Schema migration: gist_url, gate_verdict, gate_score columns on pipeline_artifacts

commit.message:
Adiciona integração TrackFlow CPF API para consulta completa

Implementa consulta completa de CPF via API TrackFlow no painel admin,
permitindo análise detalhada de crédito com dados enriquecidos.

Backend:
- Novo endpoint GET /api/cpf/trackflow/:cpf (admin only)
- Validação de CPF e tratamento de erros específicos
- Timeout de 30s para requisições
- Token TrackFlow protegido em variável de ambiente

Frontend:
- Novo componente ConsultaCPFCard com UI expansível
- Exibe dados cadastrais, financeiros, endereços, contatos
- Alertas de segurança para credenciais vazadas
- Histórico de empregos, veículos e parentes
- Loading state e tratamento de erros com toast
- Tema Tubarão (zinc-900, dourado)

Integração:
- Adicionado em pages/admin/Requests.tsx
- Nova função consultarCPFTrackFlow em apiService
- Variável TRACKFLOW_API_TOKEN configurada no servidor

Update schedule from weekdays at 6 AM to every 2 hours every day for
all Jira triage caller workflows (lisa, typescript, rails templates).

Add missing Jira triage workflow section and activation instructions to
the typescript GITHUB_ACTIONS.md template, including required secrets,
repository variables, and step-by-step setup guide.

🤖 Generated with Claude Code

Co-Authored-By: Claude

* feat(skill): add create-only workflow schedule sync to update-projects

Since create-only workflows (like claude-nightly-jira-triage.yml) are
not overwritten by Lisa updates, existing projects retain stale cron
schedules. Add step 11 to compare and update create-only workflow
schedules against the current Lisa templates during batch updates.

🤖 Generated with Claude Code

Co-Authored-By: Claude

* docs(ci): remove JIRA_BASE_URL and JIRA_USER_EMAIL from secrets table

These are repository variables, not secrets. They were incorrectly listed
in the "Jira Integration Secrets" section, which conflicts with the
Repository Variables table and the activation instructions that correctly
show them as vars.* in the workflow.

🤖 Generated with Claude Code

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(ci): add CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN to Jira triage activation docs

The reusable-claude-nightly-jira-triage.yml workflow requires both
CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN and JIRA_API_TOKEN secrets. The activation
docs were incomplete — they only listed JIRA_API_TOKEN, which would
leave users with a scheduled workflow that fails at runtime because
the Claude secret was never configured.

🤖 Generated with Claude Code

Co-Authored-By: Claude

* docs(ci): remove JIRA_BASE_URL and JIRA_USER_EMAIL from root secrets table

These are repository variables (vars.JIRA_*), not secrets. They were
incorrectly listed in the Jira Integration Secrets table in addition to
being correctly listed in the Repository Variables section, which would
cause users to configure them in the wrong place.

🤖 Generated with Claude Code

Co-Authored-By: Claude

commit.message:
fix(ux): isMitred checks laminationMethod not thicknessMm, fix 40mm edge filtering (#442)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01Cn9Wfm7uoFeR83WSQnf8mF

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(source-manager): stop cleanup job from deactivating active youtube sources

Root cause: the cleanup job marks sources inactive after 5 minutes without a
DB update. No listener ever updated overlay_chat_sources.updated_at, so all
sources were being deactivated every 5 minutes.

Fixes:
1. Raise cleanup stale threshold from 5m → 24h (emergency stop-gap)
2. Add POST /sources/activate endpoint to source-manager
3. YouTube listener calls ActivateSource when poller starts, keeping the
   source marked active in the DB

This makes the YouTube source persist as active across cleanup cycles until
a proper listener heartbeat mechanism is implemented across all services.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
